baseline_comparison.interpretation: UAD provides zero value over random sampling from the same cluster

token_level_mutual_exclusivity.description: Absorption features fire on different tokens and never co-occur in natural text
token_level_mutual_exclusivity.evidence: Feature 11513 fires only on 'three', feature 24189 only on 'four'-'eight'
token_level_mutual_exclusivity.impact: UAD's co-occurrence clustering cannot place mutually exclusive features in the same cluster

clustering_degeneracy.description: With 50 clusters on 504 features, each cluster has ~10 features. GT absorption features are distributed across different clusters.
clustering_degeneracy.evidence: Only 1/7 GT pairs ended up in the same cluster by chance
clustering_degeneracy.impact: Hierarchical clustering based on co-occurrence is the wrong tool for detecting absorption

phi_coefficient_irrelevance.description: Phi coefficient measures co-occurrence correlation, but absorption features have near-zero or negative phi
phi_coefficient_irrelevance.evidence: Absorption features are mutually exclusive (phi ~ 0 or negative)
phi_coefficient_irrelevance.impact: Phi-based filtering removes actual absorption pairs instead of keeping them

proposed_alternative_approaches.name: decoder_weight_similarity
proposed_alternative_approaches.description: Use cosine similarity of SAE decoder weights instead of co-occurrence
proposed_alternative_approaches.rationale: Features that absorb the same parent concept should have similar decoder directions
proposed_alternative_approaches.feasibility: High - decoder weights are readily available

proposed_alternative_approaches.name: causal_intervention
proposed_alternative_approaches.description: Zero out child features and measure parent feature recovery
proposed_alternative_approaches.rationale: True absorption implies causal dependence between child and parent features

proposed_alternative_approaches.name: semantic_similarity_clustering
proposed_alternative_approaches.description: Cluster features by decoder weight similarity instead of activation co-occurrence
proposed_alternative_approaches.rationale: Absorption is a structural property, not a co-occurrence property

honest_assessment.finding: UAD fails because co-occurrence clustering is fundamentally the wrong tool for detecting hierarchical absorption
honest_assessment.root_cause: Absorption features are mutually exclusive at the token level - they fire on different tokens representing different child concepts. Co-occurrence-based methods detect features that fire TOGETHER, not features that fire on mutually exclusive instances of a parent concept.
implications: UAD may work for detecting synonym features or contextually related features that co-occur frequently
For hierarchical absorption, methods based on decoder weight similarity or causal intervention are more appropriate
The collision rate proxy metric IS valid (r=0.87 with true absorption), but UAD's clustering approach cannot exploit it
